metadata.title: Guideline on Follow-Up of Patients Administered with Gene Therapy Medicinal Products
metadata.notes: 12-page CHMP guideline on clinical follow-up for gene therapy medicinal products. Applies to both clinical trial subjects and patients administered authorised GT products. Complementary to EMEA/149995/2008 (Safety and Efficacy Follow-up – Risk Management of ATMPs).

rules.rule_text: Clinical follow-up must be conducted for all patients treated with gene therapy (GT) medicinal products, including both clinical trial participants and patients receiving authorised GT medicinal products. The clinical follow-up is required to detect early or delayed signals of adverse reactions, prevent clinical consequences, ensure timely treatment, and gain long-term safety and efficacy information.
rules.source_section: Executive Summary / 4. Follow-up of patients administered with GT medicinal products
rules.verbatim_quote: This guideline is describing recommendations for clinical monitoring and follow-up after treatment with Gene Therapy (GT) medicinal products in order to detect early or delayed signals of adverse reactions, to prevent clinical consequences of such reactions and to ensure timely treatment and to gain information on the long-term safety and efficacy of the intervention. The principles laid down in this guideline are applicable for patients enrolled in clinical trials using GT medicinal products and for patients administered with authorised GT medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: For all GT medicinal products with new active substances, a risk management plan must detail the measures envisaged to ensure follow-up of efficacy and adverse reactions, with additional specificities for ATMPs as described in the Guideline on Safety and Efficacy Follow-up – Risk Management of ATMPs (EMEA/149995/2008).
rules.verbatim_quote: As for all medicinal products with new active substances, a risk management plan should detail the measures envisaged to ensure such follow up, with additional specificities for advanced therapy medicinal products described in the guideline on safety and efficacy follow-up – risk management of advanced therapy medicinal products (EMEA/149995/2008).

rules.rule_text: The marketing authorisation holder (MAH) or sponsor of a clinical trial with a GT product must ensure that traceability data on the sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storing, transport and delivery to the point of use are maintained in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007 (Art. 15). In case of MAH bankruptcy or liquidation without a legal entity taking over, the traceability data must be transferred to EMA.
rules.source_section: 4. Follow-up of patients administered with GT medicinal products
rules.verbatim_quote: The marketing authorisation holder or sponsor of a clinical trial with a GT products shall ensure that traceability data on the sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storing, transport and delivery to the hospital, institution or private practice where the product is used, are in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007 (art. 15), and upcoming guidelines related to ATMP Traceability and Good Clinical Practice. In case of bankruptcy or liquidation of a marketing authorization holder without a legal entity taking over the marketing authorization, the traceability data will be transferred to EMEA.

rules.rule_text: A risk stratification system must be applied when planning the risk management plan and follow-up for GT medicinal products in both clinical trial applications and marketing authorisation applications. Risk factors to be assessed include: chromosomal integration potential, latency/reactivation capacity, inadvertent replication capacity, persistence of expression, replication competence/incompetence, recombination/re-assortment potential, altered host gene expression, biodistribution to non-target organs, and interactions with concomitant treatments.
rules.source_section: 4.2. System for the decision on intensity and duration of clinical follow-up
rules.verbatim_quote: When planning the risk assessment of GT medicinal products and the risk management plan for GT medicinal products, the risk stratification system described in Table 1 could be applied. It is recommended to consider each of the individual risk factor for the GT medicinal product in a clinical trial application or in a marketing authorisation application. Then it should be possible to design a risk stratification profile for the GT medicinal product.

rules.rule_text: The duration of clinical follow-up observations must be sufficient to observe subjects for risks that may be due to the characteristics of the product, the nature and extent of exposure, and the anticipated time of occurrence of delayed adverse reactions. If new information becomes available during a trial or post-marketing that affects the extent and length of follow-up, the applicant must change the risk stratification and implement this in a revised follow-up plan.
rules.source_section: 4.3. Recommendations for clinical follow-up after treatment with GT medicinal products
rules.verbatim_quote: The duration of clinical follow-up observations should be sufficient to observe the subjects for risks that may be due to the characteristics of the product, the nature and extent of the exposure, and the anticipated time of occurrence of delayed adverse reactions. If additional information of importance for the extent and length of clinical follow-up is becoming available during a clinical trial or post-marketing, then the applicant should change the risk stratification and implement this in a revised follow-up plan.
rules.exceptions: Clinical follow-up period should only be extended as long as feasible and clinically relevant

rules.rule_text: For GT clinical trial pharmacovigilance, the following complementary information must be collected in Annual Safety Reports (investigational products) or Periodic Safety Update Reports (marketed products): mortality data with attempts to obtain biopsy for RCV testing; incidence and nature of new/recurrent malignancies; incidence and nature of opportunistic and serious infections; immunogenicity-related reactions; and further safety endpoints relevant to disease and target organ.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to the information required to be included in the Annual Safety Reports for GT investigational products or in the Periodic Safety Update Reports for marketed GT products, the following complementary information shall also be collected: Mortality [if patients die during the observation period, attempts should be made to obtain biopsy material to perform assay for replication competent retrovirus...] Development of any new/recurrent cancer [the incidence and nature of malignancies...] Development of infection [the nature and incidence of opportunistic and serious infections...] Immunogenicity related reactions...

rules.rule_text: For patients treated with viral vectors that can integrate or have potential for latency followed by reactivation, a monitoring plan is recommended with brief clinical history and sample testing at: pre-treatment, 3, 6, and 12 months after treatment for at least 5 years, then yearly until data indicate no further risk. Retention samples from each time point must be stored for five years.
rules.source_section: 4.3.1. Viral vectors which can integrate or have the potential for latency followed by reactivation
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that patients enrolled in clinical GT medicinal product trials, where non-clinical tests or evidence from other clinical trials using identical vectors or modifications of vectors indicate a potential for integration or late re-activation have a monitoring plan with a brief clinical history and sample testing at the following time points: pre-treatment, 3, 6 and 12 months after treatment for at least 5 years, and then yearly until data indicate that there is no longer any risk to be followed. [...] Retention samples from each time point have to be stored for five years, in order to allow further testing if a relevant adverse event appears during the follow-up period.
rules.exceptions: If all samples negative during first year, remaining samples can be archived

rules.rule_text: If any post-treatment samples for GT medicinal products are positive — indicating integration or re-activation — or if clinical evaluation indicates a treatment-induced side-effect or adverse event, a more regular and extensive clinical follow-up must be undertaken.
rules.source_section: 4.3.1. Viral vectors which can integrate or have the potential for latency followed by reactivation
rules.verbatim_quote: If any post-treatment samples are positive, indicating integration or re-activation, or clinical evaluation indicate a treatment induced side effect/adverse event, then a more regular and extensive clinical follow-up should be undertaken.

rules.rule_text: The method used to monitor vector persistence must be directed towards detection of vector sequences (e.g. PCR). Validated methods must be used to analyse samples. In special cases where sampling does not allow detection of vector sequences, monitoring may be performed by detection of expressed product.
rules.source_section: 4.3.1. Viral vectors which can integrate or have the potential for latency followed by reactivation
rules.verbatim_quote: The method used to monitor persistence should be directed towards the detection of vector sequences (e.g. PCR). In special cases (if sampling does not allow for the detection of vector sequences) monitoring could be performed by detection of expressed product. It is recommended to use validated methods to analyse the sample.
rules.exceptions: Detection of expressed product acceptable where vector sequence detection not feasible

rules.rule_text: For viral vectors without integration, latency, or reactivation potential, a monitoring plan is recommended with brief clinical history and sample testing at pre-treatment, 3, 6, and 12 months after treatment, then yearly thereafter for a minimum of 5 years.
rules.source_section: 4.3.2. Viral vectors without integration, latency or reactivation potential
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that patients enrolled in clinical GT medical product trials with viral vectors without integration, latency and re-activation potential have a monitoring plan with a brief clinical history and sample testing at the following time points: pre-treatment, 3, 6 and 12 months after treatment, and then yearly thereafter for a minimum of 5 years.
rules.exceptions: If vectors are modified to reduce known risk of delayed adverse reactions and supported by data, long-term follow-up can be reassessed

rules.rule_text: For GT clinical trials using plasmids, a monitoring plan is recommended with brief clinical history and sample testing at pre-treatment, 3, 6, and 12 months after treatment, then yearly for a total of 5 years. If prolonged gene expression or increased integration capacity is indicated, a prolonged follow-up observation period must be performed.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended that patients enrolled in clinical GT medical product trials with plasmid have a monitoring plan with a brief clinical history and sample testing at the following time points: pre-treatment, 3, 6 and 12 months after treatment, and then yearly thereafter for a total of 5 years. [...] if they have a prolonged expression of the gene or have been modified and non-clinical tests indicate an increased integration capacity, then a prolonged follow-up observation period for adverse reactions should be performed.
rules.exceptions: Clinical gene therapy trials using plasmids considered lower risk

rules.rule_text: For genetically modified human cells, the clinical follow-up must follow the recommendations applicable to the gene vector used unless non-clinical or clinical data indicate a need for a different follow-up regimen. A safety monitoring plan must be developed to cover the risk profile of the product type.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended, that the clinical follow-up after treatment with genetically modified cells follow the recommendations for the gene vector used unless non-clinical or clinical data indicate a need for a different follow-up regimen. If applicable, a safety monitoring plan should be developed to cover the risk profile of this product type as identified in non-clinical safety evaluations or from clinical experience with similar products types.
rules.exceptions: Different follow-up regimen acceptable if justified by non-clinical or clinical data

rules.rule_text: In the marketing authorisation application for a GT medicinal product, the applicant must outline a plan for follow-up of efficacy and adverse reactions. Efficacy follow-up plan does not have to include all patients but can be based on a sample of the treated patients.
rules.source_section: 4.4. Follow-up of efficacy of gene therapy medicinal products in postmarketing approval settings
rules.verbatim_quote: In the marketing authorisation application, the applicant shall outline a plan for follow-up of efficacy of GT medicinal products and of adverse reactions thereto. The principles of presentation of such data are described in the Guideline on Safety and Efficacy Follow-up Risk Management of Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products.
rules.exceptions: Efficacy follow-up can be based on a sample of treated patients

rules.rule_text: The marketing authorisation holder of a GT medicinal product must establish and maintain a traceability system allowing the individual product to be traced through sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storing, transport and delivery to the point of use. The MAH must keep traceability data for a minimum of 30 years after the expiry date of the product, or longer if required as a term of the marketing authorisation.
rules.source_section: 4.5. Pharmacovigilance, risk management and traceability in postmarketing approval setting
rules.verbatim_quote: The holder of a marketing authorisation for a GT medicinal product shall establish and maintain a system ensuring that the individual product can be traced through the sourcing, manufacturing, packaging, storing, transport and delivery to the hospital, institution or private practice where the product is used. The marketing authorisation holder shall keep these data for a minimum of 30 years after the expiry date of the product, or longer if required by the Commission as a term of the marketing authorisation. (Regulation (EC) No 1394/2007 – Art. 15).
rules.exceptions: Commission may require longer retention period

rules.rule_text: The hospital, institution or private practice where a GT medicinal product is used must establish and maintain a system for patient and product traceability. The system must contain sufficient detail to allow linking of each product to the patient who received it.
rules.source_section: 4.5. Pharmacovigilance, risk management and traceability in postmarketing approval setting
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition, the hospital, institution or private practice where the GT medicinal product is used shall establish and maintain a system for patient and product traceability. That system shall contain sufficient detail to allow linking of each product to the patient who received it.

rules.rule_text: A risk management plan must be submitted with the marketing authorisation application for GT medicinal products, in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines.
rules.source_section: 4.5. Pharmacovigilance, risk management and traceability in postmarketing approval setting
rules.verbatim_quote: With the marketing authorisation application, a risk management plan has to be submitted in accordance with the current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines (see Guideline on Safety and Efficacy Follow-up – Risk Management of Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products).

rules.rule_text: For GT medicinal products where chromosomal integration presents a risk for malignant transformation due to insertional mutagenesis, measures implemented to reduce this risk must be verified if less or no follow-up is proposed. Long-term clinical follow-up is required for vectors showing persistent signal without being integrated into the genome due to potential for latency and reactivation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chromosomal integration of vectors is considered to present a risk for malignant transformation of cells due to insertional mutagenesis and activation, inactivation or alteration of host cell genes... Measures implemented to reduce the risk of insertional mutagenesis should be verified, if less or no follow-up is to be proposed. For vectors that show a persistent signal without being integrated into the genome, propensity of the vector to undergo latency and reactivation will determine if a long-term clinical follow-up is required.

rules.rule_text: If clinically relevant antibody and cell-mediated immunity testing is required for GT medicinal product follow-up, it must be included in the clinical trial and the observation period must be sufficient to detect a signal. Where immunogenicity monitoring for a GT product reveals antibodies interfering with vector or expression protein activity, this must be flagged as a potential efficacy concern and assessed for autoimmunity risk.
rules.source_section: 4.3. Recommendations for clinical follow-up — Immunogenicity related reactions
rules.verbatim_quote: If it is clinically relevant antibody and cell mediated immunity testing shall be a part of the clinical trial and the observation period should be sufficient to detect a signal... Antibodies interfering with the activity of the gene vector or expression protein might lead to a lack of efficacy (in case it is desired to have continuous gene expression) and they can cross-react with the endogenous protein in cases where endogenous protein is still produced. In this case the consequence would be autoimmunity.
rules.exceptions: Screening not needed if antibody is non-neutralising and not targeting epitopes linked to protein activity
